title: The History of Chocolate
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Chocolate has a fascinating history that (0) back thousands of years. The ancient Maya and Aztec civilizations were the first to (1) the potential of cacao beans. They used them to create a bitter drink that was (2) to be sacred. When Spanish explorers (3) across this beverage in the 16th century, they brought it back to Europe. Initially, chocolate was only (4) to the wealthy due to its high cost. However, the Industrial Revolution made chocolate more (5) to ordinary people. Today, chocolate is enjoyed worldwide, and the industry is (6) billions of dollars annually. Modern chocolate comes in many forms, from dark to milk to white varieties. Scientists have also discovered that dark chocolate may have health (7), including improving heart function. Despite its long journey from ancient ritual drink to modern (8), chocolate remains one of the world's most beloved foods.

example.explanation: 'Dates back' is the correct collocation to refer to something that originates from a period in the past.

questions.explanation: 'Recognize the potential' is the correct collocation meaning to identify or realise the potential of something.
'Considered to be' is the correct passive structure to express that something was viewed in a certain way.
'Came across' es un phrasal verb que significa encontrar algo por casualidad.
'Available to' is the correct expression to indicate that something is within reach of a group of people.
'Affordable' means reasonably priced and is the most appropriate term in this formal context.
'Worth' followed by an amount expresses the value of something. 'Is worth billions' is the correct structure.
'Health benefits' is the standard collocation to refer to benefits for one's health.
'Treat' significa algo especial que se disfruta, perfecto para describir el chocolate como placer moderno.
